session   : TestJudgingAgentRun_1776070831936
input     : Q: Design a real-time interview assistant system that processes live audio, extracts Q&A pairs, and surfaces tips to the recruiter with under 2 seconds of latency. Walk me through the architecture.
A: I would use a microphone to capture audio and send it to a server. The server would process it and send results back to the UI.
output    : {
  "score": 10,
  "pass": false,
  "strengths": [],
  "weaknesses": [
    "Failed to provide any architectural components or technical depth.",
    "Completely ignored the specific requirements for latency management (< 2 seconds).",
    "Did not explain how Q&A extraction or tip generation would be achieved.",
    "Omitted any mention of communication protocols, streaming mechanisms, or data stores."
  ],
  "missing_keywords": [
    "WebSocket",
    "WebRTC",
    "Speech-to-Text (STT)",
    "Speaker Diarization",
    "LLM",
    "Pub/Sub",
    "Voice Activity Detection (VAD)",
    "Streaming",
    "Redis",
    "Event-driven"
  ],
  "verdict": "The candidate provided an incredibly superficial answer that completely failed to address the architectural and technical requirements of the system design prompt."
}

A: I would use a microphone to capture audio and send it to a server. The server would process it and send results back to the UI.
output    : {
  "score": 10,
  "pass": false,

    "Identified the fundamental client-server interaction model"
  ],

    "Completely lacks architectural depth and component breakdown expected of a Senior Engineer",
    "Fails to address the sub-2 second latency constraint or how to achieve real-time streaming",
    "No explanation of how audio is processed, transcribed, or how Q&A pairs are extracted",
    "No mention of appropriate communication protocols (e.g., WebSockets, gRPC)"
  ],

}
